skill.name: langchain-architecture
skill.description: Design LangChain applications with agents, chains, memory, retrieval, callbacks, testing, and production readiness patterns.
skill.summary: Plan robust LangChain applications using documented patterns for agents, memory, retrieval, callbacks, and testing.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, and network reconnaissance patterns, but review found these are false positives from markdown code fences and LangChain terminology. The file is guidance-only documentation with Python examples and no executable installer, shell command, secret access, prompt injection, or data exfiltration behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command detections correspond to triple backtick markdown fences around Python examples. They are documentation delimiters, not Ruby backtick execution or shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each cited location is a markdown fence or Python documentation example. No executable Ruby or shell command context exists in the skill file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections occur on descriptive LangChain text and agent constants. No hashing, encryption, cipher selection, or credential handling code is present at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic API use at the cited lines. The lines contain prose or LangChain agent configuration terms.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Reconnaissance Pattern
low_findings.description: The network reconnaissance detection is a use-case bullet about AI agents with tool access. It does not instruct port scanning, host discovery, probing, or unauthorized network enumeration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is high-level product documentation. No reconnaissance commands, targets, ports, or network scanning libraries are referenced.

content.value_statement: Teams need repeatable patterns when building LLM applications with agents, retrieval, memory, and tools. This skill provides structured LangChain guidance for planning workflows, callbacks, testing, and production checks.
actual_capabilities: Explains LangChain agent, chain, memory, document processing, and callback concepts.
Shows patterns for retrieval augmented generation, custom tools, and sequential chains.
Compares memory approaches for short, long, windowed, entity, and vector-backed conversations.
Provides callback guidance for logging, latency monitoring, error handling, and token tracking.
Includes testing examples for agent tool selection and memory persistence.
Lists production checklist items such as validation, rate limiting, observability, and fallback strategies.
limitations: It is documentation guidance, not a runnable LangChain project scaffold.
Some referenced support files are listed in prose but are not included in the scanned file structure.
Examples may need updates for current LangChain package versions and import paths.
It does not provide deployment automation, credential management, or hosted monitoring integrations.

use_cases.title: Plan a RAG Application
use_cases.description: Map loaders, splitters, vector stores, retrievers, and answer chains before implementation.

use_cases.title: Design Tool-Using Agents
use_cases.description: Choose agent types, define tool descriptions, and plan safeguards for external actions.

use_cases.title: Review Production Readiness
use_cases.description: Check memory growth, callbacks, testing, rate limits, validation, and fallback strategies.

prompt_templates.title: Explain the Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the LangChain architecture skill to explain the main components I need for a simple question answering app.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Memory Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the LangChain architecture skill to compare memory options for a support chatbot with long conversations.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a RAG Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the LangChain architecture skill to design a RAG workflow for internal documents, including retrieval and testing steps.

prompt_templates.title: Audit an Agent Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the LangChain architecture skill to review my tool-using agent design for observability, error handling, and production risks.

output_examples.input: Design a RAG app for policy documents.
output: A recommended component plan covering document loading, chunking, embeddings, vector storage, retrieval, and answer generation.
A testing checklist for retrieval quality, source handling, and answer reliability.
Production notes for observability, rate limits, fallback behavior, and prompt versioning.

output_examples.input: Help me choose memory for a customer support chatbot.
output: A comparison of buffer, summary, window, entity, and vector memory options.
A recommended approach based on conversation length, privacy requirements, and cost limits.
Risks to monitor, including token growth, stale context, and sensitive data retention.

output_examples.input: Review my LangChain agent before production.
output: A review of tool descriptions, error handling, callback coverage, validation, and timeout controls.
A prioritized readiness checklist with gaps and suggested next steps.

best_practices: Choose the smallest agent or chain pattern that solves the workflow clearly.
Add callbacks early for logging, token tracking, latency monitoring, and error visibility.
Test tool selection, retrieval quality, memory behavior, and fallback paths before release.
anti_patterns: Do not let agents call powerful tools without validation, limits, and review.
Do not keep unlimited conversation history in memory for long-running chats.
Do not ship retrieval workflows without checking chunking, source quality, and answer grounding.

faq.question: What does this skill help me build?
faq.answer: It helps plan LangChain applications that use agents, chains, memory, retrieval, callbacks, and testing patterns.

faq.question: Does it create a complete project?
faq.answer: No. It provides architecture guidance and examples, but it does not include a full runnable project scaffold.

faq.question: Can it help with RAG design?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers document loading, splitting, vector stores, retrievers, and retrieval question answering chains.

faq.question: Does it cover production concerns?
faq.answer: Yes. It lists error handling, logging, token monitoring, timeouts, rate limits, validation, and fallback strategies.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file is documentation and examples only, with no executable installation scripts or hidden network behavior.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
